name: Numbers to Ten Thousands — 4 and 5 Digit Numbers
whatIsIt: After hundreds come THOUSANDS. A 4-digit number like 3,482 has thousands, hundreds, tens and ones. A 5-digit number like 47,315 adds ten-thousands. Each place is 10 times the one to its right. We read, compare, add and subtract these bigger numbers using the same place-value ideas as before.
whyNeeded: Populations, prices of gadgets, distances between cities, cricket crowd sizes — the real world is full of 4- and 5-digit numbers. Reading and working with them confidently is essential everyday maths.
realLifeUses: The price of a phone or fridge (₹12,499)
The number of people at a big cricket match
Distance in metres between two towns
The population of a small town

vocabulary.meaning: Ten hundreds; the 4th place from the right.
Ten thousands; the 5th place from the right.
What a digit is worth because of its position.

story.title: The Stadium Count
story.text: Coach tells Arjun the stadium seated 4,208 fans on Saturday and 3,675 on Sunday. 'How many over the two days?' he asks. Arjun lines up the places — thousands under thousands, ones under ones — and adds: 4,208 + 3,675 = 7,883. 'Line up the columns and it's just like adding small numbers,' he grins.
story.extractedProblem: Saturday 4,208 fans, Sunday 3,675 fans. How many in total over both days?
story.answerInStory: 7,883 — add with the columns lined up.

visual.caption: 3,482 = 3 thousands + 4 hundreds + 8 tens + 2 ones. Each place is 10× the one on its right.

teachingGallery.title: Build and compare big numbers
teachingGallery.note: Read from the left; compare the highest place first.

examples.caption: The 0 tens still holds the place.

standardMethod.summary: Group digits in place-value columns; to add or subtract, line up the columns and work right to left, regrouping when needed.
steps: Write the number in columns: ten-thousands, thousands, hundreds, tens, ones.
To compare: check the highest place first; the bigger digit there wins.
To add/subtract: line up the columns and work from the ones, carrying or borrowing as usual.

commonMistakes.mistake: Not lining up the place-value columns when adding or subtracting big numbers.
commonMistakes.fix: Ones under ones, tens under tens. Misaligned columns give completely wrong answers.

commonMistakes.mistake: Assuming a number with more digits is always bigger, or comparing the wrong place.
commonMistakes.fix: First compare the number of digits; if equal, compare from the HIGHEST place downward.

easy.q: In 3,482, what is the digit in the thousands place?
hintLadder: Thousands is the 4th place from the right.

challenge.q: A town has 12,450 people; another has 9,875. How many MORE in the first town?
Ravi says 9,999 is bigger than 12,000 'because it has more nines'. What's the fix?

options.label: 12,000 has more digits, so it is bigger

challenge.answer: 12,000 has more digits, so it is bigger

questions.q: In 47,315, which digit is in the ten-thousands place?
Before adding two 4-digit numbers you must…

teachBackPrompt: Find a 4-digit price at home (a gadget, an appliance) and a smaller one. Explain to a grown-up the place value of each digit in the bigger number, then add or subtract the two prices with the columns lined up.

revisionCard.summary: Thousands and ten-thousands extend place value: each place is 10× the one on its right. Compare by digit-count, then from the highest place. Add/subtract by lining up columns and working right to left, carrying/borrowing.

meta.curriculum: CBSE/NCERT Class 4 (4- and 5-digit numbers, place value & operations)
